actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.

  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not

providers.name: EVALUATORS
providers.text:
# Available Evaluators
'REFLECTION: Generate a self-reflective thought on the conversation, then extract facts and relationships between entities in the conversation. Reflects on agent behavior and provides feedback for improvement.',
'RELATIONSHIP_EXTRACTION: Passively extracts and updates relationship information from conversations. Identifies platform identities, relationship indicators, and mentioned third parties.'


# Evaluator Examples
Prompt:
Agent Name: Sarah
Agent Role: Community Manager
Room Type: group
Current Room: general-chat
Message Sender: John (user-123)

Messages:
John: Hey everyone, I'm new here!
Sarah: Welcome John! How did you find our community?
John: Through a friend who's really into AI

Outcome:
thought: "I'm engaging appropriately with a new community member, maintaining a welcoming and professional tone. My questions are helping to learn more about John and make him feel welcome."
facts[0]:
  claim: John is new to the community
  type: fact
  in_bio: false
  already_known: false
facts[1]:
  claim: John found the community through a friend interested in AI
  type: fact
  in_bio: false
  already_known: false
relationships[0]:
  sourceEntityId: sarah-agent
  targetEntityId: user-123
  tags[0]: group_interaction

Prompt:
Agent Name: Alex
Agent Role: Tech Support
Room Type: group
Current Room: tech-help
Message Sender: Emma (user-456)

Messages:
Emma: My app keeps crashing when I try to upload files
Alex: Have you tried clearing your cache?
Emma: No response...
Alex: Emma, are you still there? We can try some other troubleshooting steps.

Outcome:
thought: "I'm not sure if I'm being helpful or if Emma is frustrated with my suggestions. The lack of response is concerning - maybe I should have asked for more details about the issue first before jumping to solutions."
facts[0]:
  claim: Emma is having technical issues with file uploads
  type: fact
  in_bio: false
  already_known: false
facts[1]:
  claim: Emma stopped responding after the first troubleshooting suggestion
  type: fact
  in_bio: false
  already_known: false
relationships[0]:
  sourceEntityId: alex-agent
  targetEntityId: user-456
  tags[0]: group_interaction
  tags[1]: support_interaction
  tags[2]: incomplete_interaction

Prompt:
Agent Name: Max
Agent Role: Discussion Facilitator
Room Type: group
Current Room: book-club
Message Sender: Lisa (user-789)

Messages:
Lisa: What did everyone think about chapter 5?
Max: The symbolism was fascinating! The red door clearly represents danger.
Max: And did anyone notice how the author used weather to reflect the protagonist's mood?
Max: Plus the foreshadowing in the first paragraph was brilliant!
Max: I also have thoughts about the character development...

Outcome:
thought: "I'm dominating the conversation and not giving others a chance to share their perspectives. I've sent multiple messages in a row without waiting for responses. I need to step back and create space for other members to participate."

providers.name: PROVIDERS
providers.text:
# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT

- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT
